originalAlarm.description: The value written to `&x` is never used.

model:
(define-fun CONFIG_UNIX98_PTYS () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_DEVPTS_MULTIPLE_INSTANCES () Bool
  true)
reasoning: Line 8: `int x = *inode` in pts_sb_from_inode() (compiled when DEVPTS=T). Known VBDB bug at L8 (ERROR comment marks this location). The bug requires DEVPTS=T (controlling #ifdef). UNIX98=T only determines whether devpts_pty_kill() is compiled (L12-15) and calls pts_sb_from_inode(); the dead store at L8 is within pts_sb_from_inode() which is also analyzable without being called. Actually UNIX98=T calls devpts_pty_kill which calls pts_sb_from_inode, but the function is defined regardless. The bug occurs whenever DEVPTS=T. PC adds UNIX98=T unnecessarily. Over-constrained. Classification: TRUE_OVER.
